trend: High-volatility correction within a longer-term uptrend. S&P 500 at 6,632 — down ~3% on the week and ~2.7% YTD — as JPMorgan warns a sustained oil price above $90 could trigger a further 10-15% correction. Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, WTI surged past $98, and Goldman Sachs raised its Brent target to $110 for March. VIX is trading near 28, up over 30% in the past month.
sentiment: Decidedly risk-off and stagflation-fearful. EUR/USD slipped to $1.14 on safe-haven USD demand. Asian equities lost $15B in a single session as Goldman slashed MXAPJ growth forecasts. Bearish Sentiment reading confirmed by Perplexity Finance dashboard as of March 15 close.

fx_prices.USD_index_note: DXY bid as safe-haven flows accelerate on Iran war escalation

fed_policy: FOMC meets March 17-18. Rates widely expected held at 3.5-3.75%. Powell press conference is the key risk event — dual pressure of rising oil-driven inflation and softening labour (92K jobs in Feb) creates a stagflation dilemma. February PPI prints March 18 before the decision.

next_major_catalysts.event: LULU earnings (after close), DOCU earnings (after close), FOMC begins
US February PPI (14:30 CET), FOMC decision + Powell press conference (20:00 CET), MU / WSM / GIS / JBL / FIVE earnings
ECB rate decision (13:45 CET), ACN / FDX / DRI / BABA / CCL earnings, weekly US jobless claims

strategy_adjustments.macro-volatility: Prime window. WTI above $98, VIX near 28, back-to-back PPI + FOMC + ECB cluster. Favour vol breakout trades post-data rather than pre-positioning. Reduce index longs into FOMC. XAU/USD and USOIL are the cleanest trending instruments; EUR/USD short against safe-haven USD demand is a secondary theme.
strategy_adjustments.earnings-momentum: Dense 4-day earnings cluster (DLTR, LULU, DOCU, MU, WSM, GIS, ACN, FDX, DRI, BABA, CCL). Pre-earnings run-ups carry extra risk this week given macro overlay. All pre-earnings positions must be flat BEFORE the print. Post-earnings gap plays (ORCL still live) are safer given defined entry after the binary risk has passed.
strategy_adjustments.post-shock: High-volatility regime creates fresh shock candidates daily. Focus on names with verifiable crash dates, non-structural triggers, and price now stabilising above the crash low. SE at $86 (crashed from $118 on guidance miss), DOCU at $47 (crashed from $94 high on downgrade wave), BNTX at $83 (hit 52-week low on trial failure), LULU at $158 (down 55% from 52-week high). Require 1-2 session stabilisation before entry.
strategy_adjustments.crypto: BTC is the standout outperformer — +6% on the week despite S&P -1.6%, with $800M in ETF inflows and Strategy's $776M BTC purchase. Institutional accumulation is decisive. Coinbase premium turned positive signalling US-led demand revival. Bear flag risk above $73-75K is the key technical warning to watch. ETH elevated by BlackRock staked ETF launch on Nasdaq. SOL and XRP moving but remain low-conviction in high-vol macro environment.
strategy_adjustments.pair-trades: Regime-neutral pairs are especially valuable into FOMC. Core themes: energy beneficiary vs airline victim (XOM/UAL, XOM/ALK), quality defensive vs reset growth (COST/WMT, LLY/NVO), BTC dominance over high-beta L1s (BTC/SOL), quality hyperscale vs capex-heavy cloud (MSFT/AMZN), safe-haven gold miner vs beaten discretionary (NEM/CCL).

key_risks: Iran fully closing Strait of Hormuz — 20% of global oil supply disrupted, Goldman $110 Brent target already in play
FOMC hawkish surprise on dot plot — oil-driven inflation forces fewer cuts, stagflation narrative accelerates S&P correction toward JPMorgan 10-15% warning
BTC bear flag above $73-75K resistance — technical breakdown could trigger $51K target per analysts
Asian equity contagion — Goldman slashed MXAPJ 2026 EPS by 2%, $15B outflow in one session
EUR/USD breakdown below $1.12 on sustained USD safe-haven bid
notes: All prices sourced from Perplexity Finance dashboard and cross-referenced with StockAnalysis, Investing.com, MarketBeat, and Yahoo Finance as of Sunday March 15 2026, 20:00 CET (Torino, IT). WTI crude rose 3.11% to $98.71 as Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, choking ~20% of global oil supply. Goldman Sachs raised Brent target to $110 for March. BTC posted 6% weekly gain outperforming all major equity indices, driven by Strategy $776M purchase and $767M spot ETF inflows. EUR/USD slipped to $1.14 on risk-off USD demand. JPMorgan warned oil above $90 could spark 10-15% S&P 500 correction with domino effects on EM. Federal Reserve holds rates at 3.5-3.75% expected; Powell's post-FOMC tone on stagflation is the pivotal risk event. BlackRock launched staked Ethereum ETF on Nasdaq March 14, boosting ETH institutional narrative.
